pi_items.source: Verified via CrossRef API search which confirms the existence and exact citation details of 'The Corsi Block-Tapping Task: Standardization and Normative Data' by Kessels et al. matching the claimed volume and pages.

privileged_info_summary: The task computationally generates synthetic Corsi Block-Tapping Task data during runtime via setup_task.sh. The generation algorithm uses random Gaussian distributions to model simulated spatial spans, utilizing real-world normative values (M=5.8, SD=1.0 for forward span; M=5.2, SD=1.1 for backward span) accurately referenced from Kessels et al. (2000) in Applied Neuropsychology, 7(4), 252-258. The setup script also explicitly synthesizes the contaminated participant named 'sub-X99' with 100% accuracy and flat 250ms response times, confirming the task's metadata claim. All export and verification scripts strictly require the agent's report to be placed at /home/ga/pebl/analysis/corsi_span_report.json.
